A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Geeta

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-02-12 · 7 answers
I read Geeta several times.Finally I found more confusion after more reading.
user [246] · 2008-02-12
When Pandavas followed the right path (,shown by Bhagwan Sri Krisna) Then why they went for Prayshit at the end of Mahabharat War ?,
user [29] · 2008-02-13
That is the nature of transcendental literature. The more one reads it the more nectar and realizations he gets ;-)
user [166] · 2008-02-13
Do you chant the Hare Krsna mantra regularly? It helps if you are, then you will be able to understand and not get confused.
user [2] · 2008-02-13
interesting question....
still millions of people were killed to restore Yudhistira to the throne, so they had to atone.
another consideration is that they have to give example to society.
Any explanation not born of my (poor) reasoning?
Please keep posting your doubts for our advancement through krishna katha!
user [38] · 2008-02-13
When Krishna killed Arista, gopis forced Him to atone by bathing in the Ganga. He agreed - although He cant be touched by papa but for the sake of dharma - and created Manasi Ganga lake. To me, this is a similar case.
user [33] · 2008-02-13
Please read Bhagavad-gita As It Is with purports of His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. These purports will help to clear up many confusions. Also, as was stated in another post in this thread, each time you read you will gain more nectar and realizations. So please read over and over again.

Hare Krishna
user [19] · 2008-02-17
